desc:
The small trail snakes through the forest between big beeches and old oaks. It
leads to the Old Forest Road to the north, deeper in the forest to the south. An
even smaller path leads to the west and disappears in the thick forest. Lots of
trunks are covered by moss, and toadstools are growing everywhere in this humid
forest.
name: Small Forested Passage

desc:
One dead tree towers up through the canopy and opens a big hole through which
light can flood down to the forest floor. The bark has at some places peeled
away from the trunk, forming handholds and allowing one to climb up the tree.
Except for this tree, all the rest here are alive and fully grown, with big lush
leaves. Lots of threads of spider-silk join some trees. The trail leads around
the dead tree to both north and west while the brambles and vines get denser in
other directions.
name: Corner of Mirkwood

desc:
The upper part of the tree is littered with the remains of an ancient spider
web. This tree-top hideout is covered with white, silky spider webs displaying
an elaborate design which is both beautiful and deadly. At this altitude the
mountains of Mirkwood can be seen to the northeast.
name: A Spider's Lair

desc:
The trees in this area have thinned out enough that sunlight is able to
penetrate to the forest floor. Due to the increase in light the ground here is
covered with a vast array of mosses and ferns. As well as a multitude of small
vegetation, a myriad of larger plant life is also present. In short, the forest
floor in this area is covered in a moist carpet of green plant life.
name: The Mirkwood Forest

desc:
The forest has now become dense with dark old larches. The ground is littered
with small dead branches and leaves which lie lifeless on the dark rich soil of
the forest floor. Brambles have overwhelmed this area, making it hard to
traverse. About twelve yards above the forest floor, leaves and branches are
intertwined into a dark, thick layer of foliage which almost completely blocks
sunlight from reaching the forest floor.
name: The Dark Forest of Mirkwood

desc:
This colossal trunk was probably uprooted by a storm, and crushed down all the
nearby trees on its way to the ground. The soil its fall threw up made a steep
ramp that rains have compacted. Further up the trunk, the sky is visible through
a gap in the foliage. To the south, the forest gets too dense to allow you to
leave in this direction. Neither to the west nor to the east is there any way to
climb off the trunk, but it might be possible to clamber over the branches to
the west, down towards the base of the tree. Between the trunk and the foliage a
large number of spider webs spread out wide. A few of them still have partly
decaying victims in them.
name: On a Giant, Fallen Trunk

desc:
The forest here is so thick and dense that all sunlight is blocked by the
towering canopy high above. The ground is full of thistles and thorns, making
movement tough and tiresome. One distinct anomaly is present: there is a massive
uprooted tree lying to the west, its bulky, cumbersome roots hanging in the air
and blocking the passage to a dark hole which opens up inside the hollow tree.
The tree and the roots are covered in spider eggs of all sizes varying in size
from a fist to a pumpkin.
name: In Front of Giant Roots

desc:
There is a small open space in the shade of a giant trunk of an ancient, fallen
oak tree. The only way out of here would be to the west, if one were to head
there, it looks very unlikely that one could come back, since the sharp branches
are angled and point west, making it far easier to go than to return. Partly
covering the ground are the skeletons of snakes in various sizes; notable are
the long, thick chain-like spines.
name: Amongst Tangled Branches

desc:
The forest has become incredibly dense. The forest floor is made of roots,
stumps and a lot of big rocks covered by moss. A natural trail weaves its way
between trees and rocks. To the south the forest is too dense to allow anyone to
pass through. To the east, jumbled heaps of branches nevertheless leave some
room to move. To the north the forest seems even darker, if that were be
possible, and definitely menacing.
name: Deep within Mirkwood

desc:
The majority of the trees in this area have one unique quality: they are
branchless. As well as lacking branches, these trees are extremely tall. Their
canopies reach so high into the sky that the tree-tops appear to spin due to the
high winds at such an altitude. To the east the forest appears to grow darker
and more dense as bushes and other forest floor vegetation reemerge. To the
north trees become less numerous but the vegetation in this area becomes vast
and dense, creating a thicket which presents a major problem to the
unexperienced traveller.
name: Branchless Forest

desc:
Beech trees abound in this corner of the forest, amidst dry ground covered with
small green bushes. A narrow largely overgrown trail leads toward the north. The
other part of the trail has been reclaimed by the forest and no hints of it can
be seen. Some trunks of the large beech trees here have a purple sheen to the
bark. To the west of the faint path, there is a large anthill and some rocks.
name: Overgrown Path through Mirkwood

desc:
This thicket is dark and dense, with masses of shrubs and bushes which reach
five feet into the air. A small part of the forest floor is visible, with small
tufts of grass hiding between the bushes and shrubs. Some beech trees fight for
ground with the thicket. The ground is soft and damp; branches from bushes reach
out and scrape the traveller. To the south, the forest changes: the trees there
lack any branches, giving an impression of openness.
name: A Dense Thicket

desc:
The ground here is wet from the moisture which lingers in the air. The soil is
black as night, a sign that this is rich healthy soil for growing things, and
indeed an array of plants thrives on the forest floor, ranging from beautiful,
colourful flowers to simple green and black molds. To the south the forest gains
strength as it becomes denser and more treacherous. Traces of what may have been
a forest trail can be seen on the ground, though it is now almost completely
overgrown by an abundance of undergrowth.
name: Between Thick Branches

desc:
There are so many fallen trees here that the ground is scarcely visible. Huge
branches, half-rotten and agglomerated with old leaves and soil, form crude
gangways over and around the fallen trunks. Young trees have sprouted under the
remains of the older ones and are already growing a new layer of foilage to
conceal this green and grey landscape. To the east, it is possible to crawl
under the dead branches and enter a weird cave. The cave has dusty old spider
webs hanging like drapery in the entrance, partly concealing it. To the south
and to the north, the forest is too dense to allow anyone through. In the west,
there are less branches and trunks, and a trail is visible under the dead
leaves.
name: The Twisted Forest Floor

desc:
There is a wide open space inside this dead, fallen tree. The interior walls
have been emptied of its pith by insects which left only the very hard bark. To
the east the trunk ends at a network of tangled roots, and there is a small
crater under the stump. Inside the crater lies a spider's victim, mummified in
webbing. To the west, just before leaving the bark, the wood seems burnt, maybe
due to a lightning strike. The border between the trunk and the branches to the
west is not easy to see.
name: Inside a Tree

desc:
Huge trees form a thick tangle of branches overhead, letting in little light.
The thick bark of the trees gives them a wrinkled and old appearance. Faint
traces of mist drift out of the layers of leaves on the forest floor. At places
there are hints of an old, mostly overgrown trail that seem to disappear beneath
the tangled growths of thorny bushes to the west.
name: Dark Forest

desc:
The surrounding forest is composed primarily of large black firs. The trees look
strange: they have no needles, and their dark barks have been disfigured. Some
trunks have fallen down, while others have strange roots which intertwine with
each other and dig deep into the forest floor, making travelling very difficult.
Numerous spider webs litter the canopy. The forest opens slightly to the east
and south where the trees are mainly oaks and beeches.
name: A Menacing Forest

desc:
A huge, flourishing tree stands tall among dead but still menacing-looking black
trees. Its branches, decorated by the remains of spider webs, are very
impressive and seem to move slightly independently of wind's blowing. There is
some open space to the west while to the other directions the vegetation looks
too dense to allow anyone to pass easily through.
name: A Baleful Forest

desc:
An odd fog engulfs the forest floor. Thick blankets of warm fog push out and
wrap around the passerby as they seem to glide over this forest floor anomaly.
To the touch, the fog is oddly warm and moist. Underneath the white covering,
the forest floor is damp and covered with molds and fungi which leach off the
moisture in the air. To the west the fog appears to rise from the forest floor
up into the forest, reaching high into the canopy. In the south the fog
dissipates and a faint opening can be seen.
name: Foggy Tree Line

desc:
Giant, oozing cocoons and a number of slimy, pulsating egg sacks are sitting in
a gross mixture of slime, spider silk and rotten vegetation. This sheltered,
stinking nest has been completely covered with a wall of entangled spider webs
from all sides save to the south, where a dark tunnel leads out of this place.
Above, the dark canopy of Mirkwood with its numerous spider webs overlooks the
area.
name: Gruesome, Stinking Nest

desc:
A cylindrical tunnel snakes along inside the canopy. Its sturdy, solid walls
consist of an entangled mess of branches, twigs and black vines, everything
having been glued together with spider silk and some kind of sticky, grey paste.
The tunnel splits, passageways leading towards the north, east and up.
name: Tunnel inside the Spider Webs

desc:
The tunnel inside the canopy makes a long curve towards the west and south.
Travel is hindered by large amounts of trash and stinking filth which cover the
floor. Along the way, there are several remains of dead animals, some of them
wrapped in rotten cocoons, accompanied by unidentifiable organic tissue splashed
on the silky, grey walls.
name: Dark-grey Tunnels

desc:
High above the ground and an unknown distance below the tree tops, this small
platform is located in the ever-dark canopy of Mirkwood. It is based on a few
sturdy branches, with vines, leaves and spider silk as construction material. A
tremoring spider web has been woven between the trees, providing an unstable-
looking bridge to the south, crossing the area in the air.
name: Platform above a Wide Chasm

desc:
A small platform has formed on the lower tree branches, offering an unblocked
view to the ground. The canopy continues without a break in all directions,
forming a black, sheltering roof above the forest floor. Below, a great oak tree
has fallen to one side, its crown still stretching up to the canopy, its trunk
leaning diagonally against the nearby trees. The trunk itself is broad and
gnarled, providing a passageway between the ground and the canopy.
name: Above a Leaning Trunk

desc:
Tightly-growing, shadowy trees result in an intact canopy that does not expose
the ground to the sky above. Low branches and shrubs are covered in dark lichens
and bracket fungi. The trees are covered in spider webs and spider nests, their
small inhabitants patiently waiting for prey to become ensnared. There is a
faint trail running to the east and south that shows evidence of regular use.
name: Gloomy Forest

desc:
A faint smell of decomposition pervades the area. A gloomy, oppressive silence
hangs here underneath the forest canopy. Spider webs can be seen covering the
high and low branches of the trees, mingled with the dark lichens that are
abundant in this part of the forest. Trampled vegetation to the north suggests
the passing of creatures on a regular basis.
name: Gloomy Forest

desc:
Some ancient trees were cut down around the area, forming a round clearing in
the forest. A small shelter stands here, two entrances to the building being
visible. Leftovers and carcasses littering the ground attest to the lack of
cleanlinesss on part of the inhabitants. Dense vegetation prevents passage to
the south. To the west a faint trail, a result of the somewhat frequent passing
of creatures in that direction, is barely discernible.
name: Hidden Courtyard

desc:
This unclean, filthy, and stinking room serves as a place to prepare meals:
there is a blood-stained table, a blackened fire pit, and a rather large, and
very dirty, pot to cook meat in. The floor is covered with half-rotten bones and
other litter, the origin of which is not clear. The crude albeit sturdy walls
are made of logs and clay. The only properly-made thing in this pigsty is the
door: made of heavy material and equipped with an iron lock, it seems capable of
keeping unwanted visitors out.
name: Crude Kitchen

desc:
A rudimentary-looking tunnel runs under the ground. The floor is well-trampled,
its clay surface being smoothed and packed down over time so that it is now hard
as rock, and there is neither litter nor loose gravel on it. At the western end
of the tunnel steep stairs, made of logs and a few bigger rocks, lead to the
upper floor, and to the east the passageway continues deeper into the earth. The
ceiling is relatively high, and shows signs of erosion.
name: Rough Tunnel

desc:
The walls of this room are made mostly of branches, dirt and rocks. The flimsy
ceiling, providing some protection from the elements, seems to be designed
mostly with the intent of providing shade rather than keeping out any kind of
precipitation. Some blankets, filthy and covered with twigs and leaves, can be
seen scattered across the room. An acrid smell permeates the room, betraying the
lack of cleanliness of its inhabitants.
name: Barracks

desc:
Inside, the shelter is dark and crude, with little care taken to keep the place
clean. The walls, made mostly of thick trunks, branches and rocks seem to be
barely held together by an occasional application of thick, dark mud. The
remains of a small fire can be seen in the centre of the room. Scattered on the
floor are the remains of past meals, being finished off by small scavenging
insects and rodents.
name: Dirty Guardroom

desc:
The tunnel has been constructed with rough efficiency. The marks left by picks,
hammers and shovels are still visible on the clay walls, and only occasionally
have the builders had time and motivation to erect poles supporting the ceiling.
The tunnel splits up to the west, east and south.
name: Junction in a Rough Tunnel

desc:
Carelessly-assembled, crude shelves line the walls of this small, damp room.
There are some containers, lumps of meat and miscellaneous small items on
shelves. The ceiling is upheld by several rough pillars of wood, into which pegs
have been hammered so that items can be hung from them. The stench of mould and
rotten meat hangs in the air.
name: Dirty Pantry

desc:
This roughly-built passage runs in a north to south direction inside the earth.
The walls and the ceiling have been reinforced with occasional logs, otherwise
the the walls consist only of hard, dark clay. The floor is smooth, as though
there were regular travel back and forth. To the north, the tunnel leads to a
junction. There is some kind of separate room to the west. To the south, the
tunnel ends at a sturdy wooden door.
name: Rough Passage

desc:
This is a small room with arched ceiling and walls constructed of crude bricks.
It is being used as a storage room, and is filled with miscellaneous items such
as crates and barrels. Alas, the moisture and rot have taken their toll, and
old, decayed junk is mostly all that remains. A thick layer of dust covers the
floor and all other surfaces.
name: Storage Chamber

desc:
This room is a strange mixture of pompous decoration and filthiness. It seems to
be some sort of command post. In the middle of the room stands a desk, with some
maps and other small equipment on it. There is an empty space around the table,
and the other furniture - a weapon rack, bed and cabinet - stands against the
walls around the room. The walls are covered with colourful tapestries whose
colours are very different from each other, and many of them are dirty and
stained. A huge, rotten spider's head has been nailed to one wall.
name: Inner Quarters

desc:
A skilfully camouflaged tunnel has been dug under the surface of Mirkwood. The
exit leading upwards is hidden under a fallen trunk and giant, black ferns, and
the other end of this small passageway ends at an underground door to the north.
Next to the door is a small peephole in the wall. The other walls and the floor
of the tunnel are made of the soil itself.
name: Escape Passage

desc:
The branches of dark and gloomy trees hang overhead under a shadowy canopy.
Gigantic, gnarled tree trunks and dense undergrowth prevent passage in any
direction save to the east. Large fungi grow here among decomposing branches and
logs, surrounded by a dense, continuous mat of black ferns.
name: Gloomy Forest

desc:
This part of the dark forest contains a large number of oaks and beeches.
Mushrooms grow in clusters between the beeches, taking full advantage of the
humid environment that hastens the decomposition of the abundant leaf litter in
the area. To the south the foreboding shadows of Mirkwood grow darker with
dense, thorn-covered underbrush making passage all but impossible.
name: Gloomy Forest

desc:
Gigantic branches, devoid of leaves, reach down from the canopy. Huge trunks,
covered in lichens and black shelf fungi, grow cluttered to the west, leaving no
space for passage. The forest floor is uneven at best with many roots having
grown through the tree litter that covers the ground. Among the decomposing
trees, button fungi of different kinds thrive.
name: Gloomy Forest

desc:
The forest here is as black as night. Trees are densely packed within this area.
The forest floor is completely void of all plant life; small animal tracks can
be seen on the soft, moist ground. The trees in this area are twisted together
in a manner that makes a disorienting view. The black trees look the same all
around, making it even more difficult to find a way through. In all directions
an entangled mass of branches and roots is visible, and seemingly prevents going
anywhere save to the east.
name: Blackened Forest

desc:
The forest remains dark, and the ground lacks any form of life outside of the
annelids and insects which swarm over most of the forest floor. The stench of
wet dirt and decaying leaves is heavily present in the air. Above the forest
floor giant trees block out the sun, while large vines and roots protrude from
it, making passage through this dark terrain very dangerous.
name: Perilous Depths

desc:
Several big, thick trees stand close to each other. Their rough, hardened grey
bark seems old and makes the trees look almost invulnerable. Leaves and twigs
lie scattered on the ground. The trees have thick branches just a few feet above
the forest floor making the travelling hard. A large nest can be seen far up in
one of the trees and the branches around it is so dense the sky cannot be seen.
name: Forest Floor

desc:
The branches overhead make this area very dark. Strands of moss hang from above,
nearly touching the ground. To the south, two especially large trees stand next
to each other, their branches crossing each other regularly and forming a
natural ladder. The ground is covered in a thick black mud, which smells of
herbs.
name: Deep in the Forest

desc:
Red-barked trees dominate the forest in all directions except west. A purple
lichen grows on the south-facing trunks. The air has a acrid, salty smell to it.
The ground is quite dry here, with dry brown underbrush and loose leaves that
stir in the wind. An occasional faint humming noise comes from the southeast
corner.
name: Corner of Mirkwood

desc:
This is a small chamber, covered with a waxy substance on the walls in a
hexagonal pattern. A large buzzing noise seems to come from everywhere at once.
The floor is sticky and slopes towards the north. Something sticky drips from
the ceiling. Sunlight reaching the room makes it glow a dull amber colour.
name: Inside the Hive

desc:
Up here in towards the top edge of the canopy, some new leaves grow upon the
black twisted branches. Three large branches come together here, forming a
natural platform that seems fairly safe to stand on. On the the black and brown
bark you can see insects crawling. A rare sight opens up above, at places one
can see the sky between the uppermost branches. The air up here is fresh.
name: On the Dark Branches

desc:
A dark misty woodland continues uninterupted in all directions. Tall slender
trees of various kinds stand side by side, their trunks forming a continuous
wall of dark wood, their crowns creating an intact canopy above. Black oaks with
twisted, old branches seem to grasp at anything passing through here while dead,
dried-out birch trees stand still with their old branches looking like pointing
fingers. Leaves of different sizes and colours cover the ground and make a
crunching sound when walked upon.
name: Misty Woodland

desc:
This dark bower deep within the forest looks amazingly like a cave, a strange
cave where the surfaces are of living wood, not stone; for the roof and walls of
this gloomy cavern-like arbour are formed by the branches of the trees, all
twisted densely together.
name: The Cave-like Bower

desc:
Threads of sticky, thick spider web have been spun from the upper canopy of the
forest to form a swinging bridge two yards wide which spans across the open air,
stretching between the branches of two huge oaks. The ground level of the forest
looms through the vines and branches far below, where trees and thorn-bushes
surround a small open area, as if trying to conceal and suffocate it.
name: An Arching Web Bridge

desc:
A small platform opens up upon the branches, centred around a web-covered, black
tree trunk. There is a hole in it, through which one could descend to the lower
levels of the canopy. The only other exit is along a stretched spider web,
swinging in the air like a rope bridge, leading across the open air to another
tree to the north.
name: Platform above a Wide Chasm

desc:
A circular tunnel crawls and zigzags through the canopy, occasionally sloping
upwards, sometimes downwards. Its walls are made of skilfully woven spider silk
which creates an elastic, sticky barrier around this space, denying even a
glimpse of the outside and forcing one to stay inside the tunnel.
name: Web-covered Tunnel

desc:
Spider webs surround this area like a huge, grey- and silver-coloured veil,
covering everything with its suffocating embrace. Inside this sticky shroud,
some worn-out tunnels exist, creating causeways between the old, worm-infested
trees. Here, the tunnels meet each other, forming a junction inside the canopy.
name: Intersection within the Canopy

desc:
A narrow tunnel crawls through the dense, web-covered vegetation, winding around
the tree trunks and twisting as it follows the branches upon which it is built.
A pool of dried blood stains the floor of this cramped passageway. A strong,
foul stench hangs in the stale air, getting stronger the further the tunnel
climbs.
name: Foul-Smelling Gangway

desc:
Dark, thorny vines hang from the darkness like limp, hairy tentacles. The vines,
black leaves, branches and ever-present spider webs are all tangled together,
creating an almost impenetrable wall of mess around this small platform. The
only ways through this barrier are a black hole to the west, leading further
into the canopy, and another hole at the bottom of the landing, leading down to
the ground of the shadowy forest.
name: Platform of Webs and Branches

desc:
The tunnel slopes quite steeply in a north-south direction, ascending towards
the south. The tunnel floor is very uneven. Sometimes the web-covered surface
rises abruptly up, forcing the traveller to crawl over the obstacle, other times
it sinks down, forming deceptive hollows - many of them being full of litter and
foul-stinking slime.
name: Stinking Tunnel

desc:
Judging from the amount of corpses, bones and broken armour lying about, this
gloomy tunnel could almost be an abandoned battlefield. A thick layer of spider
webs and stinking, gooey slime covers it all, mixing everything into
disgusting-looking grey porridge. Within the slime, several huge egg sacks can
be seen, woven from white silk. The stench of gore, rot and slime hangs heavily
in the air.
name: Cocoons, Carcasses and Slime

desc:
A small hollow has been formed out of the leaves here, with dried grasses
pressed into the mud. The walls and floor of this small space are covered with a
mixture of rotting leaves and various mosses, creating an obnoxious, disgusting
aroma. Large worms crawl through this putrid stuff. The odour of entrails is
distinct from that of rotting leaves.
name: Inside the Rotten Leaves

desc:
A group of black firs bare of needles stand next to each other, forming a half
circle around this small, oppressive glade. The branches stick out like spears;
they are strong and sharp, and seem to reach out as if trying to stab careless
travellers. Beyond and above the firs, the vegetation intermingles together,
blocking both any view of the sky and any movement save to the west.
name: Thick Forest

desc:
Snaking through the forest a small pathway passes a peculiar-looking group of
jet black trees. A thick layer of black lichens covers the whole area, giving
the trees and rocks their, even for this forest, unusually dark colour. Densely
growing trees are spreading their twisting branches and tangled vines are
hanging from the canopy as if trying to prevent any movement away from this
oppressive place.
name: Blackened Trees

desc:
A riotous mass of dense shrubs surrounds the dark trees to the north, seemingly
blocking one's way in that direction. To the east a sharp ridge rises, its side
covered with the same type of shrubs that grow to the north, making it
impossible to get to the top of the ridge from here. A small pathway leads to
the south and west through the dusky forest, the part going south looking a lot
more used.
name: Gloomy Forest

desc:
Large trees covered in dark mosses, lichens and fungi crowd the area. The
vegetation that surrounds the trees grows denser and denser to the south,
effectively blocking passage in that direction. There are broken branches and
twigs in the shrubs to the north, as well as lightly trampled vegetation,
providing evidence that living creatures do travel in that direction on a
regular basis.
name: Gloomy Forest

desc:
The forest is very dark and cold here, and the trees are like black giants that
look as if they extend infinitely toward the sky. The trees seem to whisper to
each other through their slow, rhythmic creaking. High up in the branches,
small, silk-wrapped cocoons can be seen hanging.
name: Whispering Mirkwood

desc:
The forest is quite dense and the branches from the immense oaks have formed a
seemingly impenetrable canopy overhead. Along the trees and on the ground there
is a blanket of spider-infested cobwebs. Numerous knotted roots snake across the
forest floor which might make unwary travellers stumble.
name: Sticky Path in the Forest

desc:
The forest here is pretty thick and dense, and the sheer quantity of vines
hanging down from the huge oaks makes it even darker. The vines seem to protect
the old trees from danger and almost appear to react to movement within the
forest. Between two giant oaks to the north, the carcass of some long-dead
creature is suspended from snaking vines several feet above ground. In a line
running from north to south, the forest floor appears to have been disturbed.
The disturbance disappears to the south through a large cobweb that is strung
between trees.
name: Brushy Forest

desc:
A set of steps have been carved into the rock at the eastern end of this tunnel.
The steps lead up to a slab that blocks the exit. The tunnel runs through
crumbling and cracked rock and the walls are reinforced with stones of irregular
shapes. While the craftmanship seems lacking, the mason was aware of his lack of
skill for the ceiling is supported by thick beams of roughly-hewn wood at even
intervals.
name: Rock-walled Tunnel

desc:
Ominous, black yew trees grow in this area. As a result of that, many rotting
piles of leaves lie undisturbed on the ground. High up in the foliage, there are
hundreds of cobwebs and threads hanging and swaying slowly in the wind. The
dense foliage allows almost no light through.
name: Deep in the Forest of Mirkwood

desc:
The trees here are too dense to let any light through and because of that there
is barely any undergrowth at all, except some sort of fungi growing here and
there. A white and foul-smelling wall barricades the way to the east. The wall
seems to be made out of rotten trees and a lot of cobwebs.
name: Dense Forest

desc:
There are cobwebs woven all over the trees and there are spiders everywhere. It
is hardly possible to set down a foot here without crushing a spider. To the
east stands a great archway formed from stooped trees and masses of thickly
knotted cobwebs. From the archway hang several thick, sticky strands of
spider-silk, in contrast to the other cobwebs these seem to have been freshly
spun. Some pattering and squeaking noise can be heard originating from beyond
the archway.
name: Forest of Cobwebs

desc:
The forest is fairly bright here but it is not because of any break in the
canopy. All around are strands of silver-white webs covered in beads of dew that
glitter and sparkle. Everywhere on the ground thousands of small spiders scurry
about or lurk in tiny webs. These small spiders are likely newly hatched and, in
time, will grow much larger.
name: Forest of Cobwebs

desc:
Thick, white cobwebs coat every visible surface and numerous spiders can be seen
crawling over them, maintaining their sticky traps. The black oaks that grow
here have become twisted and bent and now lean over the smaller plants as if
threatening their very existence. An overpowering foul stench emanating from the
east engulfs the whole place.
name: Forest of Cobwebs

desc:
Silky webs cover most of the walls in this cavern, leaving only small sections
of dark stone visible. In some of the webs, huge and bulky spider sacs are
suspended. Every now and then a sac quivers and trembles as if the spiders
inside were excited by the presence of prey in their hatchery.
name: Spider Hatchery

desc:
Broken, crumbled cocoons of various sizes and shapes litter the floor, while
those that look fresh hang suspended from the ceiling or cling to the walls. A
dark, sticky liquid is seeping from some of the newer cocoons where they hang
from the ceiling by strands of silvery spider-silk. The fluid has begun to pool
on the ground in repulsive pools of liquified flesh.
name: Gruesome Feeding Cave

desc:
A strong smell of death permeates the air throughout this part of Mirkwood. The
forest is very dense, and dark trunks tangled with thick undergrowth form a
small, enclosed area. This area is used as a den by giant spiders, judging from
several corpses that are hanging from branches by thick white cords. The forest
floor is littered with bones and sharp, jagged rocks, all completely immersed in
a sea of cobwebs.
name: Gruesome Spider Lair

desc:
This side of the outpost has a crude gate blocking the passage to the north,
keeping the animals of the forest and other creatures and potential enemies out
of the outpost. In addition, the orcs have a good control of the passage of this
part of forest with the strategic placement of the gate, and the other
structures of the outpost, such as a high stockade and crude huts.
name: North Gate of an Outpost

desc:
An area next to the main outpost has been fenced in by a solid-looking fence of
thick, crooked branches and smaller trunks. The enclosed area is located between
three big pine trees and the outer palisade encircling the whole outpost. Inside
the area, there are many gnawed bones and one bloody, half-eaten carcass of an
ox-like animal lying on the ground.
name: By a Kennel

desc:
A huge fire pit has been dug in the centre of this area. Around the pit, there
are several logs serving as primitive benches. Sticks, bones, and pots lie
scattered on the ground. A big cauldron is hanging above the fire pit, with some
foul-smelling concoction inside. Several severed appendages, looking
suspiciously like those of the great spiders of Mirkwood, stick up from the
cauldron and hang over its edge.
name: Fire Pit

desc:
At the centre of the outpost, there is an open area with no huts or other
buildings. Several wide trees have been left standing here providing some shade
while their lower branches and surrounding undergrowth has been cleared away. A
variety of objects, such as broken shields of wood, rotting carcasses of
spiders, and other junk have been nailed to the trunks. Most objects have been
thoroughly demolished by arrows and slingstones, and it is obvious that this
area is used for practicing ranged weaponry.
name: Untidy Target Range

desc:
In the midst of the trees, this miserable orkish outpost offers protection from
malicious creatures of the forest outside thanks to the high wooden palisade.
Some of the trees behind the palisade have been hacked down, but not so much as
to let the sun shine through. A rope ladder is hanging from a wooden platform
visible up in a pine tree, which is overgrown by black vines.
name: Below a Watchtower

desc:
Several shoddy huts have been constructed here, with roofs of branches and
vines, and walls made of mud and wood. A small pit for a camp fire is located
between the huts, with a few roughly-made chairs standing around the fire pit.
To the west, there is a strong wooden palisade protecting the outpost. The
apparently indifferent inhabitants of the huts have discarded trash and food
leftovers on the ground.
name: At the Outpost's Barracks

desc:
Several filthy mats and blankets lie scattered on the floor of this miserable
hut. There are no windows in the walls and only one door leading out. The roof
is made of branches and vines, held up by a few rough but sturdy beams. It is
sealed with mud in an attempt to make it weatherproof. The walls are built in a
similar manner.
name: Inside a Barrack

desc:
The crude worksmanship of this hut leaves little doubt that it is a work of
orcs. Even so, more effort has gone into it than would be typical; it is likely
the quarters of an important orc. The hut stands on a foundation of stone slabs
that, even though they are uneven, is further evidence of the effort gone into
its construction. Several furs, skulls, and other trophies hang on the walls,
and a thick mat of pelts in the centre of the room might serve as a bed, if
somewhat oddly placed.
name: A Captain's Corner

desc:
This end of the outpost has an opening in the palisade, but it is blocked by a
huge pile of garbage. The pile is obviously working as the final location for
trash, filth, bones, and food leftovers of the inhabitants of the outpost. Very
strong, foul odours waft from the huge garbage pile. A palisade is blocking the
way to the west and east.
name: Garbage Pile

desc:
Around the outpost, there is a wooden palisade built from pikes and sharpened
stakes. On a few locations along the palisade there are small platforms made of
wood and stone, providing ideal positions for archers. The palisade is
uninterrupted to the south, but to the east there is a huge wooden gate
reinforced with bands of metal.
name: Orkish Outpost

desc:
A well-hidden wooden platform has been built amidst the forest canopy; it
probably serves as a lookout for watching the road that runs through the forest
to the south. There is a rack for arrows and other weapons on the centre of the
platform, and judging from the crude craftsmanship, it is of orkish origin.
Rough as the whole place may seem, it fills its purpose perfectly. A rope ladder
hangs from the hatch in the floor.
name: Lookout in the Trees

desc:
The ground is littered with leaves and sand, and some occasional gravel. The
forest behind the boulder is made of dead-looking trees with no leaves left,
apart from one healthy, thick, brown trunk leading straight up into the skies
with a big, green, luscious crown. This big brown tree seems to have a makeshift
ladder built into it. The forest is dead silent, and nothing can be heard
except, from time to time, the whistling wind.
name: Behind a Grey Boulder

desc:
On top of a small, steep hill this hideout's purpose seems to be to watch the
road down south from here. Traces of Orcs can be seen all over the place, in the
form of half-eaten animal bodyparts, torn leather armour and various other
debris. A small hammock has been wickered and woven together out of vines and
other plants, and makes a comfortable resting place.
name: Camouflaged Hideout

desc:
Several pots and casks are placed on shelves standing along the rough cave
walls. The cave is wide enough for several men to stand shoulder-to-shoulder.
This natural cave has two doors, one is a grating in the ceiling that leads up
and likely out of the cave, and the other is a solid door on the western wall.
There are several weapon racks beside the door and a chest on the floor in front
of them.
name: Storage Cave

desc:
There is no bed or other furniture in this damp, miserable section of cave. A
pile of leaves and pieces of branches lie on the floor, covered by a tattered
blanket. There is a small hole in the floor near the back wall that, judging
from the stench that rises from it, serves as a place for prisoners to relieve
themselves. A ring is fastened to the cave wall with thick but rusted iron
spikes, and a chain is attached to the ring. There are some scribblings and
scratches on the rocky walls, but they are too vague to be read. On the floor,
there are many old brownish stains.
name: In a Prison

desc:
A short wooden ladder leads from this musty rock-walled tunnel up to the surface
through a small opening. One of the wooden beams supporting the ceiling has
collapsed, forming a pile of rubble on the ground. The ceiling, where visible
above wooden beams, seems to be of loose and wet earth. Small clumps of mud-like
soil fall to the tunnel floor constantly and a collapse can not be far off. The
walls are reinforced by rocks of irregular sizes and shapes that seem doomed to
fail at staving off the inevitable collapse.
name: Rock-walled Tunnel

desc:
Behind the hedge a small, recently cut clearing opens up. The area is partly
covered in stumps, branches and leaves. This supply depot is well hidden and
offers leather armours of the finest kind, as well as various other supplies. A
sturdy bench has been assembled from a couple of logs, and a make-shift table
stands right beside the bench. Old, partly-eaten bones lie scattered across the
area.
name: Orc Supply Depot

desc:
No ray of light can pierce the dense canopy here. Many giant cobwebs hang in the
foliage above. As a result of the permanent darkness here, the vegetation on the
forest floor has died. Nothing is left on the forest floor but dead vegetation
on otherwise barren soil between the knotted and twisted roots of the dark,
looming trees.
name: Deep in the Forest of Mirkwood
